what: Restore the known-good aiter hybrid path from best_submission.py to recover from the current slow Triton regression
why: The current submission benchmarks in the hundreds of microseconds to hundreds of milliseconds per shape, far worse than the archived aiter baseline. Re-establishing the proven path is the fastest way to recover a usable kernel and resume real optimization.

what: Use a16w8 page_size=16 for bs=256,kv=1024 to reduce page metadata overhead on the worst small-KV large-batch case
why: The recovered baseline is only 0.3% behind the local best, and the benchmark gap is concentrated at bs=256,kv=1024. Doubling page_size there halves page count from 32768 to 16384 without changing the 8k path.

what: Route bs=256,kv=1024 through bf16_persist ps=64 instead of a16w8 to cut page metadata without fp8 tolerance risk
why: The a16w8 ps=16 variant failed correctness on bs=256,kv=1024. bf16_persist is already stable on large-token shapes and may recover the 256x1024 hotspot with fewer pages and no fp8 quantization error.
